sections.content: VHF (Very High Frequency) maritime radio operates in the 156–174 MHz band and is the primary means of short-range communication at sea. It uses line-of-sight propagation, meaning the signal travels in a straight line from antenna to antenna. Typical range is 20–60 nautical miles depending on antenna height and atmospheric conditions.

sections.title: Key Fact
sections.content: VHF Channel 16 (156.800 MHz) is the international distress and calling frequency, monitored by vessels and coast stations required to maintain a continuous VHF watch.

sections.prompt: What is the typical range of a VHF maritime radio?

options.text: 20–60 nautical miles depending on antenna height

sections.explanation: VHF radio uses line-of-sight propagation. Range depends primarily on antenna height at both ends, typically achieving 20–60 NM.
